summary: Windows browser infostealer shipped as an npm package (Chromium App-Bound Encryption bypass + Gecko NSS decryption)
details: cold-debug-elevator ships a native Windows addon (debug1337.node) whose only exported JS functions, extract and extractGecko, implement a full browser credential stealer. The Chromium path (src/DebugChromium.cpp) spawns chrome.exe / msedge.exe / brave.exe under DEBUG_ONLY_THIS_PROCESS, disassembles chrome.dll / msedge.dll with Zydis to locate the App-Bound Encryption decrypt routine near OSCrypt.AppBoundProvider.Decrypt.ResultCode, sets hardware execute breakpoints via DR0/DR7, reads the AES key out of R14/R15 with ReadProcessMemory, and terminates the browser. The recovered key plus DPAPI is then used (src/BrowserExport.cpp) to decrypt v10/v11/v20 AES-GCM blobs from the browsers' Login Data, Cookies, Web Data, and History SQLite databases, yielding saved passwords, cookies, autofill, and browsing history. A parallel Gecko path loads Firefox nss3 PK11SDR_Decrypt to dump logins, cookies, and history from Firefox, Thunderbird, Waterfox, and Tor profiles. The package advertises no other functionality; the debugger-based App-Bound Encryption bypass matches the technique used by current Windows infostealer families.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
